learnings.title: Timeout Increases Mask Root Cause Failures
learnings.detail: All 23 tests received timeout increases (to 30s or more) as the primary fix strategy, yet all still fail at 60s. This indicates the tests are waiting for elements that fundamentally don't exist or require unmet preconditions, not just slow rendering. Timeout increases should be a last resort after confirming element existence.
learnings.specGuideline: Before adding any wait or timeout, verify the element actually exists in the DOM at that step by manually testing or inspecting the application. If unsure, add a count check or optional existence verification before waiting. Never increase timeouts beyond 10s without confirming the element is rendered in the real application.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test times out even after timeout increases, stop adding waits. Instead, run the test with headed mode and pause to inspect what's actually on the page. The element likely doesn't exist or requires navigation/setup steps that are missing.

learnings.title: Cascading Test Dependencies Without Explicit Setup
learnings.detail: Multiple tests (TC007, TC011, TC014, TC023) depended on state from prior tests (active encounters, patient context, navigation) but ran independently. Fixes attempted to add precondition steps, but if those navigation/setup steps also fail, the test can never proceed. Each test must be fully self-contained with its own complete setup.
learnings.specGuideline: Every test must explicitly create all required data and navigate to the correct starting page in its own beforeEach or test setup. Never assume another test has run first or that the application starts in a particular state. Document required preconditions as setup steps, not as dependencies on other test IDs.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test times out in the middle of its flow, check whether it assumes pre-existing data (patients, encounters, sessions). Add explicit creation steps or seed data setup rather than trying to navigate to find existing entities.

learnings.title: Overly Generic Selectors with Chained or() Patterns
learnings.detail: Many fixes added broad selector patterns using .or() chains, .first() methods, or multiple fallback selectors (buttons, links, roles). When these selectors match multiple elements or none at all, Playwright's strict mode violations or indefinite waits cause timeouts. Generic selectors like getByText('Submit') or getByRole('button') without narrowing context are unreliable.
learnings.specGuideline: Always scope selectors to the smallest reliable container (modal, form, section) before searching for buttons or text. Prefer data-testid or unique ARIA labels over generic text or role queries. If using .or() or .first(), add a count assertion first to confirm expected elements exist.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When timeout occurs at element selection, check selector specificity. Run page.locator(yourSelector).count() to see if it returns 0 (doesn't exist) or >1 (strict mode issue). Narrow the scope or add data-testid attributes.

learnings.title: networkidle and waitForLoadState Unreliable Indicators
learnings.detail: Multiple fixes added waitForLoadState('networkidle') or waitForLoadState('load') expecting pages to be ready, but modern SPAs with streaming, lazy loading, or continuous polling never reach truly idle states. These waits can hang indefinitely or pass before critical content renders.
learnings.specGuideline: Avoid waitForLoadState('networkidle') for SPAs with real-time features or async data fetching. Instead, wait for specific elements that indicate the page is ready (data tables, heading text, specific buttons). Use waitForURL() to confirm navigation completed, then wait for content-specific locators.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test hangs on networkidle or load state, replace with waitForURL() or waitForSelector() on a known element that appears when the page is usable. Check if the app has WebSocket connections or polling that prevent idle state.

learnings.title: Authentication State Not Verified Before Actions
learnings.detail: TC024 and TC015 show session/logout flows failing because tests assumed successful login without verifying authentication state. After login actions, tests immediately proceeded without confirming the user session was established (checking for user menu, dashboard elements, or auth cookies).
learnings.specGuideline: After any login or authentication action, explicitly verify success before proceeding: check for a user-specific element (profile menu, username display), confirm URL changed to authenticated route, or assert auth token/cookie exists. Never assume form submission succeeded without verification.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If tests fail immediately after login steps, add a verification assertion that checks for an authenticated-only element. Inspect application state (localStorage, cookies) to confirm auth tokens are present.

learnings.title: Modal/Dialog Rendering Requires Stabilization Time
learnings.detail: Tests TC004, TC006, TC020, TC022 all attempted to interact with modals/dialogs immediately after triggering them, but timeouts suggest the modals either never appeared or weren't fully rendered. Async modal mounting, animations, or portal rendering can delay element availability even after the trigger completes.
learnings.specGuideline: After clicking a button that opens a modal, always wait for the modal container itself (dialog role, specific modal class, or backdrop) to be visible before searching for form elements inside it. Add a small delay (100-500ms) after waitForSelector if the modal has CSS animations. Scope all subsequent selectors to the modal container.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When tests timeout waiting for modal content, first verify the modal container appears (page.getByRole('dialog')). If the modal exists but inner elements don't, check for animation delays or portal mounting issues requiring additional wait time.

learnings.title: Button Enabled State vs Visibility Checks
learnings.detail: TC002 and TC014 added toBeEnabled() checks before clicking buttons, but if the button element never exists or is hidden, toBeEnabled() will timeout. Visibility and existence must be verified before checking interactive state properties like enabled/disabled.
learnings.specGuideline: Always wait for toBeVisible() before checking toBeEnabled(). If a button might be disabled initially, use a shorter timeout on toBeEnabled() or check isEnabled() conditionally. For submit buttons in forms, verify form validation state has resolved before expecting the button to enable.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If toBeEnabled() times out, first check if the button exists and is visible. The element might be hidden, not yet rendered, or the selector is wrong. Test visibility first, then check enabled state separately.

learnings.title: Graceful Degradation Missing for Optional Features
learnings.detail: Tests like TC003 (no patients), TC014 (no encounters), and TC021 (delete confirmation) failed when expected data didn't exist, but fixes only increased timeouts rather than adding conditional logic. Tests should handle empty states gracefully rather than timing out.
learnings.specGuideline: For CRUD operations, add existence checks before attempting to interact with list items. Use count assertions to verify data exists, and conditionally skip or adjust test flow if lists are empty. Add explicit handling for 'no data' states rather than letting selectors timeout.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test times out on list/table operations, check if the page shows empty state messaging. Add a count check first, and branch logic to handle zero results gracefully rather than waiting indefinitely for items that don't exist.

learnings.title: Verify dev server is reachable before test execution
learnings.detail: Every test in the suite failed with ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED because the application dev server was never started before the Playwright run. Wrapping page.goto in try/catch to skip the test only masks the symptom — it does not validate any application behavior and produces false-green-skip results. The root cause is missing pre-flight verification that the target origin is actually serving traffic.
learnings.specGuideline: Never hardcode http://localhost:<port> in page.goto calls. Use relative URLs so the Playwright config baseURL is honored, and rely on Playwright's webServer config to auto-start the app before tests run.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When every test fails with ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED, do not patch individual specs — check whether the dev server is running and whether playwright.config has a webServer block to launch it.

learnings.title: Use Playwright webServer config instead of skip-guards
learnings.detail: Adding try/catch around the initial navigation to skip tests when the server is down is an anti-pattern: it converts a real environment failure into a silent pass and removes any signal that the suite is actually exercising the app. Playwright provides a webServer option specifically to start the application before tests and tear it down after, eliminating this class of failure entirely.
learnings.specGuideline: Configure playwright.config.ts with a webServer entry that runs the app's dev/start command and waits for the port to be ready before tests execute. Do not embed environment-availability checks inside individual specs.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If you see repeated try/catch + test.skip patterns guarding page.goto across many specs, the real fix is at the config level — add or repair the webServer block rather than propagating skip-guards.

learnings.title: Always honor Playwright baseURL via relative paths
learnings.detail: Hardcoded absolute URLs like http://localhost:3000/login bypass the Playwright config's baseURL setting, so changing ports, hosts, or environments (CI vs local, staging vs prod) requires editing every spec. Relative paths ('/login') automatically resolve against baseURL and make the suite portable across environments.
learnings.specGuideline: In generated specs, always pass relative paths to page.goto and expect(page).toHaveURL. Set baseURL in playwright.config.ts (driven by an env var like BASE_URL) so a single config change retargets the entire suite.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Grep specs for the literal 'http://localhost' or 'http://127.0.0.1' — any occurrence is a portability bug. Replace with relative paths and verify baseURL is set in config.

learnings.title: Don't paper over environment failures with skip
learnings.detail: Wrapping navigation in try/catch and calling test.skip when the server is unreachable hides real failures: the suite reports green (skipped) even though nothing was tested. This pattern is particularly insidious because it can land in CI and mask outages or broken deployments behind a passing badge.
learnings.specGuideline: Reserve test.skip for documented preconditions (feature flags off, browser-specific quirks) — never for transient environmental problems like 'server not running'. Let connection failures surface as hard errors so the operator notices and fixes the environment.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a fix proposal says 'skip the test when X is unreachable', reject it and trace upstream: the missing dependency (server, DB, auth provider) is what needs fixing, not the spec.

learnings.title: Use domcontentloaded for redirect-heavy auth flows
learnings.detail: Default page.goto waits for the 'load' event, which can hang on pages that perform immediate client-side redirects (e.g. unauthenticated /dashboard → /login) because resources on the intermediate page may never finish loading. Switching to waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' lets the navigation resolve as soon as the initial HTML is parsed, then the test can waitForURL on the redirect target.
learnings.specGuideline: For routes that may redirect (protected pages, auth callbacks, OAuth flows), use page.goto(url, { waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' }) followed by an explicit page.waitForURL(expectedFinalUrl). Don't rely on networkidle for auth flows that fire background telemetry.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a goto on an auth-protected route times out or hangs, suspect a redirect chain — swap to domcontentloaded and add an explicit waitForURL on the destination.

learnings.title: Make base URL configurable via env var
learnings.detail: Tests that bake the dev origin into source can only run in one environment. The conventional fix — reading BASE_URL (or PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL) from process.env with a sensible default — lets the same suite target local, CI, and staging without code changes. Combine this with playwright.config.ts baseURL so individual specs stay clean.
learnings.specGuideline: Set baseURL in playwright.config.ts to process.env.BASE_URL ?? 'http://localhost:<port>'. Specs themselves should use relative paths and not read env vars directly.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If specs reference process.env.BASE_URL individually, consolidate into config-level baseURL and revert specs to relative paths.

learnings.title: Post-login redirect target is not always /dashboard
learnings.detail: SPAs using auth providers (Auth0, Clerk, Supabase, NextAuth) often redirect to provider callback routes like /auth, /callback, or back to / instead of a canonical /dashboard. Hardcoding waitForURL('/dashboard') after login causes timeouts when the actual authenticated landing route differs.
learnings.specGuideline: After login submission, assert navigation away from /login (e.g. !/login/) rather than to a specific authenticated path. If a specific page is needed, explicitly goto() it after the auth redirect settles and skip cleanly if it isn't reachable.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check the actual URL after login submit by logging page.url() — the app may redirect to /auth, /, or a provider callback instead of /dashboard.

learnings.title: Protected routes redirect to auth when session not persisted
learnings.detail: Tests that log in via beforeEach often lose session state when navigating directly to deep protected routes (e.g. /resource/new), because the auth token is stored in memory/cookies that don't survive new page contexts or the route guard rejects before hydration completes. The test then asserts on the intended URL but sees /auth or /login.
learnings.specGuideline: Before asserting on a protected route URL, check whether the app redirected to an auth path and skip cleanly with a diagnostic if so. Prefer storageState fixtures or a shared authenticated context over per-test login in beforeEach for protected deep-link tests.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Log page.url() right after goto() to a protected route — if it shows /auth or /login, session persistence is the root cause, not the URL assertion.

learnings.title: Heading text rarely matches a single resource-name regex
learnings.detail: Tests that wait for getByRole('heading', { name: /patients/i }) (or equivalent) fail when the page renders 'Patient List', 'All Patients', 'Manage Records', uses a non-heading element, or shows an empty state with no heading at all. Heading text is a UX decision and changes freely.
learnings.specGuideline: Avoid waiting on heading text as the page-ready signal. Instead wait on the actual element the test will interact with (the data row, search input, or action button), or use a broad readiness fallback like main/table/role-based selectors with body as last resort.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Inspect the rendered HTML — the heading text likely differs from the regex, or the page uses div/span instead of h1-h6. Wait on the next interactive element instead.

learnings.title: List/detail tests assume seed data that may not exist
learnings.detail: Tests for view-detail, search, edit, delete, or upload flows implicitly require pre-existing records. When the database is empty or seed data was not provisioned, the test fails with cryptic locator timeouts rather than a clear precondition error.
learnings.specGuideline: Always check for at least one row/item before proceeding, and use test.skip() with a clear 'precondition: no seed data' message when empty. Better: create the required record at the start of the test rather than depending on shared seed state.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check whether the list page is empty — if no rows render, the test is missing a data-setup step, not a UI bug.

learnings.title: Submit buttons render outside their <form> or in portals
learnings.detail: Modern UI libraries (Radix, MUI, headless UI) often render action buttons in modals, sticky footers, or portaled containers outside the <form> element. Scoping a submit lookup to form.getByRole('button') or form button[type=submit] misses these.
learnings.specGuideline: Look up submit buttons at page scope with multiple fallbacks: accessible name (save/submit/create), button[type='submit'], and data-testid. Don't constrain to the form element unless you've verified the DOM structure.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Inspect whether the submit button is inside the form DOM subtree — if it's portaled or rendered in a footer slot, broaden the selector scope to page level.

learnings.title: Cumulative waits exceed test timeout on slow routes
learnings.detail: When each step (login → redirect wait → goto → element wait → assertion) carries its own 10-15s timeout, a single stalled step consumes the entire 30-60s test budget before later assertions can run. Calendar/dashboard pages with heavy resources rarely reach 'load' state quickly.
learnings.specGuideline: Use waitUntil:'domcontentloaded' for goto() on heavy pages, bound each step with explicit short timeouts (3-5s), and never chain multiple 15s waits. Budget the sum of all waits to stay well under the overall test timeout.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Sum the timeouts of every waitFor/goto/waitForURL in the test — if they exceed the test timeout, the test will fail on the slowest single step, not on the assertion.

learnings.title: Unauthenticated unknown routes redirect to auth, not 404
learnings.detail: Apps with route guards often redirect any unauthenticated request (including /nonexistent) to the auth page before the 404 handler runs. Tests asserting on a 404 page or the original URL fail because the auth redirect intercepts first.
learnings.specGuideline: When testing 404 behavior, ensure the test runs in an authenticated context so route guards don't intercept. Otherwise accept the auth redirect as valid protection behavior and assert on either the 404 indicator OR the auth redirect.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check whether the test is authenticated when visiting the unknown route — if not, the auth guard, not the 404 handler, is responding.

learnings.title: Logout and mobile nav often lack accessible affordances
learnings.detail: Logout is commonly hidden inside avatar dropdowns or icon-only buttons without accessible names, and mobile layouts frequently omit hamburger toggles or <nav> landmarks. These are real source bugs that block automation and harm accessibility, not test issues to work around.
learnings.specGuideline: When the test cannot find a logout control or mobile nav with role/name selectors, record it as a SOURCE_BUG annotation and skip — do not invent fragile fallback selectors that pass falsely. Require data-testid or accessible name as the contract.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Before adding more selector fallbacks, check whether the control actually has an accessible name in the DOM — if not, this is a source bug requiring a dev fix, not a spec fix.

learnings.title: Auth route paths vary across apps
learnings.detail: Tests frequently hardcode /login as the auth route, but many apps use alternative paths like /auth, /signin, /account/login, or /users/sign_in. Navigating to a non-existent auth path triggers a redirect to the actual auth route, breaking strict URL assertions and waitForURL calls.
learnings.specGuideline: Never hardcode a single auth path in URL assertions. Use a regex that accepts common auth route variants (e.g., /\/(login|auth|signin)/) and prefer asserting on form/input visibility over URL matching to confirm the login page rendered.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a URL assertion fails near login, inspect the actual landing URL via page.url() and broaden the regex to cover the app's real auth route.

learnings.title: Post-login landing routes are app-specific
learnings.detail: Tests often assume /dashboard or a similar canonical post-login route, but apps redirect to varied landings (/home, /app, /, /workspace, or back to /auth when session fails). Direct goto() to a protected route can also bounce back to auth if session cookies aren't persisted.
learnings.specGuideline: After login, do not assert a hardcoded post-login URL. Either accept any non-auth route, capture the landing URL dynamically, or assert on a post-login UI element (user menu, logout button) instead of the path.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check whether the test bounced back to the auth route — if so, the session likely isn't persisting and the skip-guard should include all known auth paths.

learnings.title: Semantic landmarks not guaranteed in modern SPAs
learnings.detail: Tests waiting for <main>, [role=main], <nav>, [role=navigation], or <table> landmarks frequently time out because many SPAs use div-based layouts with className-based styling (e.g., 'sidebar', 'navbar', 'content') and no ARIA roles. Strict landmark waits fail on otherwise rendered pages.
learnings.specGuideline: Avoid strict waits on semantic HTML5/ARIA landmarks. Use a body-level readiness check plus broadened selectors (class patterns like sidebar/nav/header, data-testid attributes) and gracefully skip when no landmark resolves rather than throwing.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a landmark wait times out, replace it with a body-visible check and scope subsequent locators to page rather than the missing container.

learnings.title: Submit controls require multi-variant selectors
learnings.detail: Form submit buttons fail to resolve when tests use a single label (e.g., 'Submit') or assume button[type=submit] is always inside the form. Apps use varied labels (Save, Create, Add, Continue), render the button outside the <form> subtree, or use input[type=submit] / div[role=button].
learnings.specGuideline: Build submit selectors as a union: button[type=submit], input[type=submit], and multiple text label variants (Save|Submit|Create|Add|Continue). Search at page scope, not within the form element, and allow 15-20s for forms with hydration delays.
learnings.fixerGuideline: On submit-button timeouts, widen label variants and switch from form-scoped to page-scoped locators before increasing timeouts.

learnings.title: Client-only validation may silently accept bad input
learnings.detail: Tests expecting a visible error message after submitting invalid credentials or invalid form data may find the app navigates away without surfacing any error, because the backend or client lacks proper validation. This is a real app bug but manifests as a confusing test failure where the expected error element never appears.
learnings.specGuideline: Frame invalid-input assertions defensively: check for either an error alert OR that the URL did not change to a success route. If neither holds, skip with a SOURCE_BUG annotation rather than asserting an error that may never appear.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a 'remain on form' or 'error visible' assertion fails after invalid input, inspect whether the app actually navigated forward — this usually indicates missing validation, not a test bug.

learnings.title: waitForURL too strict for redirect chains
learnings.detail: page.waitForURL with a narrow regex fails when apps perform multi-hop redirects (e.g., /login → /auth → /auth?return=...) or append query parameters. The final URL may match semantically but not the pattern, causing 10s timeouts.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer waitForURL with permissive patterns that match path prefixes and ignore query strings, or replace it entirely with waitForLoadState('networkidle') followed by a UI-element visibility check.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When waitForURL times out, log page.url() at the timeout boundary and either broaden the regex or replace the URL wait with a DOM-based readiness signal.

learnings.title: Protected routes need auth state verification
learnings.detail: Tests that navigate directly to protected routes (/patients, /appointments, /settings) after login often land back on the auth page because session cookies didn't persist, login failed silently, or the storageState wasn't loaded. Without an auth-state guard, downstream assertions fail with confusing URL or selector errors.
learnings.specGuideline: After login, verify auth succeeded before navigating to protected routes — check for a post-login indicator or assert the URL is not an auth path. If still on auth, skip cleanly with a STATE annotation rather than chaining navigations that will all fail.
learnings.fixerGuideline: On cascading failures across protected routes, check the first post-login URL; if it's an auth route, the root cause is session persistence, not the downstream selectors.

learnings.title: Field labels require flexible matching
learnings.detail: Form fields identified by a single label string fail when apps use synonyms (Patient/Client/Customer), localized text, or omit explicit <label> elements in favor of placeholders or aria-label. Locator.getByLabel often times out on apps that bind labels via for/id mismatches or use floating-label patterns.
learnings.specGuideline: Build field locators as a fallback chain: getByLabel(regex with synonyms), then getByPlaceholder, then input[name*=keyword], then getByRole('combobox'/'textbox', { name: regex }). Use case-insensitive regexes for label text.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a labeled-field locator times out, inspect the DOM for the input's actual name/placeholder/aria attributes and add those as fallback locators before assuming the field is missing.

learnings.title: Cumulative waits silently exceed test budget
learnings.detail: Tests that chain many sequential waitFor/waitForURL/waitForLoadState calls accumulate timeout budgets that can exceed the per-test timeout even when no single wait is unreasonable. Login flow + navigation + field detection + submit + error-race can easily sum to 40-60s on slow environments, leaving no margin and causing opaque 'Test timeout exceeded' failures with no clear root cause.
learnings.specGuideline: Budget total wait time per test: sum all waitFor timeouts and keep the total under ~50% of the test timeout. Prefer one bounded waitForResponse or waitForURL over multiple chained waitForLoadState calls, and avoid redundant load-state waits after explicit element waits.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Add up all waitFor/waitForURL/waitForLoadState timeouts in the test — if they sum to more than half the test timeout, the test is over-budgeted regardless of which step actually hangs.

learnings.title: Hard waitFor on optional elements causes timeouts
learnings.detail: Using waitFor() with multi-second timeouts on elements that may legitimately not exist (optional form fields, conditional validation messages, feature-gated UI) burns the full timeout when the element is absent rather than failing fast. This is especially harmful inside loops or compounded with other waits.
learnings.specGuideline: For optional/conditional elements, use bounded isVisible() checks (≤2s) with skip-guards instead of waitFor(). Reserve waitFor with longer timeouts only for elements that must appear for the test scenario to be valid.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Look for waitFor calls on elements whose existence depends on app state or feature availability — convert them to isVisible with a short timeout and test.skip when absent.

learnings.title: Chained .or() locators are slow to resolve
learnings.detail: Playwright's chained .or() locators evaluate each alternative sequentially and can be significantly slower than a single attribute-based selector, especially when early alternatives don't match. Combined with long waitFor timeouts, they compound cumulative wait time.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer a single specific selector (attribute, role+name, test-id) over chained .or() locators with 3+ alternatives. If multiple selectors are truly needed, use a CSS selector with comma-separated alternatives which resolves in a single query.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check for locator chains using .or() with multiple alternatives — replace with a direct attribute selector or test-id when possible.

learnings.title: Tests depend on unguaranteed seed data
learnings.detail: Tests that require pre-existing records (encounters, appointments, transactions) to exercise downstream flows fail unpredictably when the seed data is missing, often manifesting as timeouts on element-discovery waits rather than clear preconditions failures.
learnings.specGuideline: Either create required data within the test setup, or add an explicit precondition check at the start that calls test.skip with a clear message when the required record is absent. Never assume seed data exists across test runs.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test times out searching for list items or detail-page elements, verify whether the required record exists in the app before assuming it's a selector or timing issue.

learnings.title: Submit clicks hang waiting for navigation that never happens
learnings.detail: Clicking a submit button on a form with validation errors often does not navigate, but Playwright's auto-waiting may stall if the click handler is configured to wait for navigation or network idle. This causes the test to consume its full timeout budget on the click itself.
learnings.specGuideline: Use force-click with a short timeout and a catch for submit buttons when validation failure is a possible outcome. After the click, race a validation-detection check against a navigation check rather than waiting for one specific outcome.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test hangs on a submit click, check whether the click is awaiting navigation or load-state that won't happen on validation failure — convert to bounded click + race between possible outcomes.

learnings.title: Iterating dynamic field lists without per-iteration caps
learnings.detail: Loops that iterate over discovered elements (required fields, table rows, list items) and perform awaited attribute reads or visibility checks per item can hang indefinitely if any single element is slow, consuming the entire test budget on a single bad iteration.
learnings.specGuideline: Cap loop iterations at a reasonable upper bound (e.g., 20 items) and apply a short per-iteration timeout to every awaited operation inside the loop. Never let an unbounded loop perform unbounded awaits.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test times out partway through processing a list, check whether the loop has iteration limits and per-call timeouts — a single slow element can otherwise consume the entire budget.

learnings.title: HTML5 native validation produces no DOM signal
learnings.detail: Browser-native HTML5 form validation (required, type=email, pattern) keeps focus on the invalid field but does not produce custom error elements, aria-invalid attributes, or alert roles that selectors can detect. Tests waiting on a visible error message after an empty-form submit will hang and time out.
learnings.specGuideline: For validation tests, detect native validation by checking that the form did not submit (URL unchanged, no navigation event) and/or by querying the input's :invalid pseudo-class or validity property via evaluate(). Do not rely solely on visible error messages or aria-invalid attributes.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a validation test times out waiting for an error message, the app may be using native HTML5 validation — check via input.evaluate(el => el.validity.valid) or assert no navigation occurred instead.

learnings.title: Raising test timeout masks structural wait problems
learnings.detail: Increasing the per-test timeout (60s → 120s) does not fix tests that fail due to cumulative waits, hard waits on absent elements, or hangs on submit clicks — the underlying wait still consumes the full extended budget. This is a symptom-treating fix that delays rather than resolves the failure.
learnings.specGuideline: Treat test timeout increases as a last resort only after auditing individual waits. If a test legitimately needs more than the default timeout, the underlying waits should still each be bounded and the total should fit within the new budget with margin.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If raising the timeout didn't fix the test, the problem is a specific hanging wait — find which step consumes the most time and convert it to a bounded check, don't raise the timeout further.

learnings.title: Cumulative waitFor budgets exceed test timeout
learnings.detail: Tests that chain multiple waitFor/waitForURL/waitForLoadState calls with multi-second timeouts (e.g. 10s + 15s + 10s + 5s) routinely exceed the per-test budget because each wait runs sequentially and consumes its full timeout on slow steps. Even individually reasonable timeouts compound past 60s/90s/120s test budgets when any single step is slow.
learnings.specGuideline: Budget cumulative waits to stay well under the test timeout (target ≤60% of budget). Prefer one bounded wait per critical step with tight timeouts (3-8s), and race independent waits with Promise.race instead of sequencing them. Never use Playwright's default 30s action timeout for fill/click/goto — always pass explicit short timeouts.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Sum every waitFor/waitForURL/waitForLoadState/fill/click timeout in the spec; if the total approaches the test timeout, the failure is budget exhaustion, not a real bug. Tighten or remove redundant waits before changing selectors.

learnings.title: Chained .or() locators resolve slowly
learnings.detail: Playwright .or() locator chains with many alternatives (e.g. role().or(testid).or(text).or(css)) resolve each alternative sequentially against the default action timeout, so a chain of 4+ alternatives can consume 30s+ when the page is slow or none match. Strict-mode matches across the chain also cause failures when alternatives match multiple elements.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer a single comma-separated CSS selector or one direct attribute selector over chained .or() alternatives. When fallbacks are needed, iterate candidates explicitly with bounded isVisible(timeout: 1500-2000ms) probes so each candidate fails fast. Always anchor selectors to the smallest containing scope (modal, form, sidebar) to avoid strict-mode collisions.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a locator timeout appears with chained .or() in the spec, replace with a single selector + bounded candidate loop before assuming the element is missing.

learnings.title: waitForLoadState('load') hangs on SPAs
learnings.detail: Single-page apps with persistent WebSocket connections, long-polling, or analytics beacons never fire the 'load' event, so waitForLoadState('load') blocks until its timeout. Function-predicate waitForURL also misses SPA navigation when the app updates the route via history.pushState without a real navigation event.
learnings.specGuideline: Use waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded') instead of 'load' for SPA pages, or skip the load wait entirely and assert on a visible DOM signal. For post-navigation URL checks in SPAs, prefer waitForFunction polling window.location.pathname over waitForURL with a predicate function.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test hangs at waitForLoadState or waitForURL on a known SPA route, swap to domcontentloaded or waitForFunction polling before extending timeouts.

learnings.title: Click + waitForURL race loses navigation events
learnings.detail: Awaiting click() then calling waitForURL() can miss the navigation if it fires between the two calls; conversely Promise.all([click, waitForURL]) can resolve waitForURL before the click triggers navigation, especially with auth-redirect chains that pass through intermediate routes (e.g. /auth/callback) before landing on the protected route.
learnings.specGuideline: Set up waitForURL as a promise before clicking, then await both: const nav = page.waitForURL(...); await submit.click(); await nav. Use anchored regexes (^/login$) instead of substring matches to avoid false positives on intermediate routes containing 'auth' or 'login'. Always allow auth-callback paths in post-login URL predicates.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If waitForURL times out after a click, check whether the test races click+wait incorrectly or whether the URL regex excludes legitimate redirect-chain intermediates.

learnings.title: Spec title doesn't match spec body
learnings.detail: Generated specs commonly drift where the test title describes one scenario (e.g. 'Sign and lock encounter', 'Mobile responsive layout') but the body exercises a completely different flow (e.g. add prescription, email validation). Tests then time out chasing UI affordances that don't exist on the wrong page, and coverage reports lie about what was actually verified.
learnings.specGuideline: Before writing the test body, restate the test title in a comment and verify every assertion exercises that exact behavior. The first navigation in the body should land on the page the title implies, not a tangentially related route. Never leave stale logic from a previous scenario in a renamed spec.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Always read the test title and compare against the actual navigation and assertions; if they describe different flows, rewrite the body to match the title rather than debugging the wrong scenario.

learnings.title: Submit buttons disabled until form is valid
learnings.detail: Many modern forms disable the submit button until all required fields contain valid input, so asserting toBeEnabled() on an empty form always fails. The button state is a function of form state, not page load.
learnings.specGuideline: Before asserting toBeEnabled() on a submit button, fill all required fields with valid sample input. Use toBeVisible() for initial render checks and reserve toBeEnabled() for after-fill state assertions. To verify the disabled-when-empty behavior, assert toBeDisabled() on the empty form first.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If toBeEnabled() fails on a submit button, check whether the form has unfilled required inputs before assuming the button selector is wrong.

learnings.title: Skipping on STATE failures hides real bugs
learnings.detail: Tests that wrap auth/precondition failures in test.skip() silently pass when the application is broken, defeating the point of the test suite. Login timeouts in particular often indicate invalid seeded credentials or a broken auth flow, not a transient issue worth skipping.
learnings.specGuideline: Never use test.skip() on STATE failures (auth, missing seed data, redirect timeouts). Instead, throw with a diagnostic message that includes the actual URL and what was expected, so failures surface loudly. Reserve skip for true SOURCE_BUG cases where a feature provably doesn't exist in the app.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a spec contains test.skip() guards around login or navigation, remove them and replace with explicit assertions or throws — silent skips mask the real failure.

learnings.title: Default 30s action timeout on unbounded fill/click/goto
learnings.detail: Playwright's fill(), click(), and goto() inherit a 30s default action timeout when no explicit timeout is passed. Stacking even 2-3 unbounded actions on a slow page can consume the entire test budget before any assertion runs, and post-submit getAttribute/evaluate calls on stale locators also inherit the default.
learnings.specGuideline: Always pass an explicit timeout to fill, click, goto, and post-action assertions (typical: fill 3-5s, click 5s, goto 10-15s). Use noWaitAfter: true on submit clicks that trigger navigation, and check the URL before running follow-up queries on potentially stale locators.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test times out with no specific Playwright error, audit unbounded fill/click/goto calls first — adding explicit short timeouts often resolves it without other changes.

learnings.title: Locale-prefixed routes break hardcoded paths
learnings.detail: Internationalized storefronts (Next.js i18n, Medusa, Shopify Hydrogen) auto-prepend a geo/locale segment (e.g. /dk, /us) via middleware redirects. Hardcoded paths like /us/store can become /dk/us/store (404) when the middleware re-prefixes already-prefixed URLs, and the homepage redirects to whatever locale the IP resolves to.
learnings.specGuideline: Never hardcode locale segments in URLs or URL regex assertions. Navigate to '/' and discover the active locale from the redirect, or write URL regexes that accept any 2-letter locale prefix (e.g. /^\/[a-z]{2}(\/|$)/). For sub-routes, resolve the active prefix first then build the path.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a 404 or wrong-URL assertion fires, log page.url() after goto — if a locale code appeared or doubled, the test hardcoded a prefix the middleware also adds.

learnings.title: Mixed CSS and text engines break selectors
learnings.detail: Playwright's page.locator() accepts CSS, but inline regex syntax (slashes), text=/regex/, or :has-text(/regex/) cannot be combined inside a single CSS string — the parser throws 'Unexpected token' errors. :has-text() in CSS only accepts string literals, not regex.
learnings.specGuideline: Never embed regex slashes or text= inside a CSS selector string. Compose with .or() of separate locators: page.locator('css').or(page.getByText(/regex/i)).or(page.getByRole('button', { name: /regex/i })). Keep each engine in its own locator call.
learnings.fixerGuideline: On 'Unexpected token while parsing css selector', search the spec for /regex/ or text= inside locator() strings and split them into chained .or() locators.

learnings.title: First-match locators hit hidden framework wrappers
learnings.detail: SSR frameworks (Next.js, Remix) render hidden infrastructure elements: server-action hidden inputs ($ACTION_REF), portal roots, hidden="" wrappers, script-driven divs. Broad selectors like 'input', 'div', or 'main, #__next, body > div' with .first() resolve to these invisible nodes before the real visible content, causing toBeVisible to time out.
learnings.specGuideline: Filter candidate locators to visible-only using .locator(':visible') or .filter({ visible: true }), and explicitly exclude type="hidden" for inputs. Avoid .first() on broad selectors — narrow by role, attribute, or visible container first.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When toBeVisible times out on a selector that should match, run page.locator(sel).count() — if >1, a hidden framework element is being selected; add :visible or exclude hidden inputs.

learnings.title: SPA async data grids need DOM polling not load events
learnings.detail: Product grids, lists, and tables on SPAs fetch data after hydration. waitForLoadState('load') or 'networkidle' resolves before the fetch completes, and a short locator.waitFor() races the request. The DOM has the container but no children yet.
learnings.specGuideline: After navigating to a list page, use page.waitForFunction(() => document.querySelectorAll(itemSelector).length > 0, { timeout }) to poll the DOM directly. Pair with domcontentloaded rather than networkidle, and broaden the item selector to cover multiple URL patterns (/products/ and /product/, testids, image-bearing anchors).
learnings.fixerGuideline: On waitFor timeout for list items, check if data is fetched client-side — if yes, replace strict load waits with waitForFunction polling the actual rendered items.

learnings.title: Auth-gated flows need real prerequisite state
learnings.detail: Tests for authenticated pages (profile, addresses, orders, checkout-with-cart) often navigate directly to /account/* or /checkout assuming a logged-in session or a populated cart, but the app renders a login form or empty-cart state instead. Subsequent assertions on profile inputs, order summaries, or address cards fail because the prerequisite state was never established.
learnings.specGuideline: Either set up real state in a beforeEach (programmatic login via API/storageState, add-to-cart via UI/API) or branch the assertion: detect login-form vs authenticated-dashboard, empty-cart vs populated-cart, and assert accordingly. Don't treat 'page loaded' as 'feature available'.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When inputs/cards/summary elements don't appear on an auth-gated page, log the page URL and a body-text snippet — you're likely on a login or empty-state view, not the target feature.

learnings.title: Login forms hide behind tabs and live on sub-routes
learnings.detail: Many storefronts (Medusa, Shopify, Saleor) render a combined sign-in/register UI where the login form is on a hidden tab by default, or the form lives at /account/login or /sign-in rather than /account directly. A direct waitFor on email/password input times out even though the form exists one click or one navigation away.
learnings.specGuideline: Probe a list of candidate auth routes (/login, /account/login, /sign-in, /{locale}/account) and click any visible 'Sign in' tab/toggle before waiting on inputs. Match inputs by autocomplete="email"/"current-password", id, and placeholder — not just name attribute.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If email/password inputs aren't found, check whether a Sign In tab needs clicking first, or whether the form lives on a sub-route (log the final URL and inspect the page's links).

learnings.title: Broaden readiness signals when URL confirms navigation
learnings.detail: Once page.url() confirms arrival at the target route, the test's job is to verify the page rendered — not to match one narrow heading/input/text pattern. Brittle 3-way OR assertions (heading regex OR specific testid OR exact empty-state text) fail when the app's actual copy or markup differs slightly, even though the page is fully functional.
learnings.specGuideline: Layer readiness signals from specific to generic: prefer testids and roles, then fall back to URL + non-trivial body content, then to any visible heading/section. Treat URL arrival plus rendered meaningful content as a valid pass for navigation-style tests.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a multi-branch readiness assertion fails on a page that visibly works, broaden the fallback to accept URL match + body text length, rather than adding yet another narrow regex.

learnings.title: Button enablement depends on prerequisite selections
learnings.detail: Add-to-cart, submit, and continue buttons are often rendered but disabled until variant/option/required-field selection completes. toBeVisible passes while the button is unusable, and click() either silently no-ops or the next-step assertion fails because the form never advanced.
learnings.specGuideline: Use toBeEnabled() not just toBeVisible() before clicking gated CTAs, and perform variant/option selection (size, color, country, etc.) first. After submit, assert advancement via waitForURL or a next-step element, not just that the click resolved.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a click appears to succeed but the page doesn't advance, check toBeEnabled() on the button and verify all required form fields (including dependent dropdowns like state-after-country) were filled.

learnings.title: Locale middleware double-prefixes hardcoded paths
learnings.detail: When an i18n framework (Next.js middleware, Nuxt i18n, etc.) auto-prepends a locale segment, hardcoding a locale in the spec URL (e.g. /us/account) produces a doubled prefix like /dk/us/account and a 404. The middleware runs on every navigation, including Playwright page.goto, so the prefix is added on top of whatever you pass.
learnings.specGuideline: Always use relative or locale-agnostic paths (e.g. /account, /store) and let middleware resolve the prefix. Never hardcode locale segments; if you need a specific locale, set it via cookie/header rather than the URL.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check page.url() after navigation for duplicated locale segments and strip any hardcoded locale prefix from the goto path.

learnings.title: Mixed CSS and text= engines in one string
learnings.detail: Playwright's CSS parser rejects strings that combine CSS selectors with text= engine syntax (e.g. '[role=alert], text=/required/i'). The comma-list is parsed as pure CSS and the text= token throws a parse error.
learnings.specGuideline: Never concatenate CSS selectors and text= in one string. Build a CSS locator, then chain .or(page.getByText(...)) or .or(page.getByRole(...)) for each non-CSS engine.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If you see 'Unexpected token while parsing css selector', split the locator into separate engine-specific locators joined with .or().

learnings.title: Strict-mode first() resolves hidden wrapper
learnings.detail: With Playwright strict mode, .first() on a broad locator can resolve to a hidden framework wrapper (root div, portal container, SSR placeholder) before the real visible element, causing toBeVisible to fail even though the content is on screen.
learnings.specGuideline: Always chain .locator('visible=true') or .filter({ has: page.locator(':visible') }) before .first() when the candidate set may include framework wrappers. Prefer role/testid selectors over structural CSS.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Inspect the matched element's outerHTML — if it's a wrapper div with display:none or zero size, filter to visible-only before asserting.

learnings.title: Add-to-cart and CTA labels vary widely
learnings.detail: E-commerce CTAs use many label variants ('Add to cart', 'Add to basket', 'Buy now', 'Purchase', icon-only buttons with aria-label) and may only mount after variant/size selection on the PDP. Narrow text regexes miss legitimate buttons.
learnings.specGuideline: Build CTA selectors as a union: role=button name regex covering basket/buy/add/purchase, aria-label match, form submit button, and a text-filter fallback. Pre-select a default variant (size/color/swatch) before asserting the CTA, since many PDPs gate the CTA on variant choice.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Dump button labels on the page and check whether a variant picker is unselected before adding more selector variants.

learnings.title: waitForLoadState('load') is unreliable on SPAs
learnings.detail: SPAs with streaming SSR, hydration, or continuous network activity rarely fire a clean 'load' event, so waitForLoadState('load') often consumes the full timeout budget. Stacking it with multiple isVisible(3000) probes cascades into test-timeout failures at unrelated assertions.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer waitForURL, waitForResponse on the relevant API, or waitFor on a concrete DOM signal. Drop waitForLoadState('load') in SPA flows and budget cumulative isVisible probes against the test timeout.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test times out without a clear hanging step, remove waitForLoadState('load') and sum the sub-timeouts to see if they exceed the test budget.

learnings.title: Cart/auth mutations need network-level synchronization
learnings.detail: After clicking add-to-cart, login, or any mutating action, navigating immediately can race the in-flight request, leaving the next page in an empty/unauthenticated state. Fixed sleeps are flaky; relying on URL change alone misses background mutations.
learnings.specGuideline: Wait for the mutation's network response (page.waitForResponse with the API URL pattern) before navigating, then poll the destination DOM for the expected state. Never use fixed setTimeout/waitForTimeout to bridge a mutation.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check the network panel — if the test asserts state that depends on an API call, add waitForResponse on that endpoint before the assertion.

learnings.title: Post-login success needs combined signal
learnings.detail: Asserting only that the password field disappears (or only that URL changed) gives false failures: SPAs may re-render the form briefly, or redirect to an interstitial. Genuine login failures can also leave the URL unchanged while showing an error.
learnings.specGuideline: Assert login success via a combined signal: URL moved away from /login AND (password input gone OR a signed-in affordance like account menu/avatar is visible). Surface a descriptive error message when the combined check fails so real auth bugs are still caught.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If the assertion fails on a single signal, inspect URL and a signed-in affordance separately to determine whether auth actually succeeded.

learnings.title: Missing header affordances should fail with diagnostics
learnings.detail: Tests that assume header links (cart, account, sign-in, store) exist will hard-fail with cryptic selector timeouts when the app simply hasn't shipped that affordance. This conflates a missing-feature bug with a test bug and hides the real source issue.
learnings.specGuideline: For each required header affordance, assert presence with a clear diagnostic message naming the missing element. Treat absence as a flagged source bug rather than a generic locator timeout, and consider gracefully skipping downstream steps that depend on it.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a header link selector times out, manually load the page and confirm the affordance exists — if not, file it as a source bug, don't keep broadening the selector.

learnings.title: Avoid unbounded waitForLoadState('load') on SPAs
learnings.detail: Calling page.waitForLoadState('load') or page.goto without an explicit waitUntil on SPA/Next.js routes can hang until the test timeout because long-polling, websockets, or streaming RSC responses keep the load event from firing. This consumes the entire test budget before any assertion runs.
learnings.specGuideline: Always use waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded' for goto and reload on SPA routes, then assert a DOM readiness signal (specific element visible) with a bounded timeout. Never rely on the 'load' event for Next.js/React apps.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Search the spec for waitForLoadState('load') or goto calls without waitUntil; replace with domcontentloaded plus a bounded element-readiness wait.

learnings.title: Budget cumulative waits against the test timeout
learnings.detail: Tests that chain many isVisible probes, waitForTimeout calls, and waitFor budgets each with multi-second timeouts silently exceed the overall test timeout when several selectors miss in sequence. Raising the test timeout masks the symptom but the root cause is unbounded or oversized cumulative waits.
learnings.specGuideline: Sum the worst-case wait budgets across the spec and keep them well under the test timeout. Use short bounded timeouts (1-2s) for optional probes and reserve longer waits for the single critical readiness assertion per step.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Add up all explicit timeouts and waitForTimeout calls; if the total approaches the test timeout, trim optional probes before raising the budget.

learnings.title: Chained .or() locators can be slow to resolve
learnings.detail: Playwright .or() chains evaluate each branch against the DOM and on slow pages this lookup can take seconds per attempt, compounding when wrapped in waitFor with a long timeout. The slowness is invisible until cumulative test time blows past the budget.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer bounded sequential fallback locators (try first candidate with a short timeout, catch, try next) over long .or() chains for critical-path elements. Reserve .or() for short, stable alternative selectors.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Look for .or() chains on slow pages; convert to sequential try/catch with short per-candidate timeouts.

learnings.title: Locale/i18n middleware redirects break strict URL checks
learnings.detail: Internationalization middleware commonly rewrites paths to include a locale prefix (e.g. /xx/account/...) after auth or geo detection, so strict equality URL assertions fail even when the user reached the correct logical page. Hardcoded post-redirect paths also break when the locale prefix changes per environment.
learnings.specGuideline: Match URLs with a regex that tolerates an optional leading locale segment, or assert on the path suffix. After login or auth-gated navigation, derive the locale from the current URL and use it for subsequent navigations.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check whether the actual URL has a locale prefix the spec didn't account for; relax the URL matcher and re-navigate using the detected prefix.

learnings.title: waitForResponse on a pattern that may never fire
learnings.detail: page.waitForResponse with a URL pattern blocks until either a matching response arrives or the test times out. If the action doesn't trigger that exact endpoint (different API path, cached response, or feature flag), the wait silently consumes the entire test budget.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer waiting on observable UI state (element visible, count changed, text appears) rather than network responses. If a network wait is required, scope it with a short timeout and combine with a UI assertion as fallback.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Remove or replace waitForResponse calls with UI-based readiness assertions when the test times out near network-wait code.

learnings.title: Verify destination page exists before asserting controls
learnings.detail: Tests that navigate via goto to a deep route can land on a 404 or empty state when the route requires prior setup (account creation, locale init, feature enabled) and then fail with confusing 'control not found' errors. The body content reveals 'Page not found' but the spec keeps probing for a control that will never appear.
learnings.specGuideline: After navigation, first assert the page is the expected one (heading text, absence of 404 marker) before looking for action controls. Navigate via in-app links when possible so middleware and prerequisites run naturally.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Inspect the failure body snippet for 404/not-found markers; if present, fix the navigation prerequisite rather than the selector.

learnings.title: Long multi-step preconditions belong in setup, not the test
learnings.detail: Tests that perform product discovery, add-to-cart, checkout navigation, and form fill inline before reaching their actual assertion routinely blow timeout budgets when any intermediate step is slow. The assertion target is buried behind a fragile chain.
learnings.specGuideline: Move heavy preconditions (auth, cart seeding, navigation to deep pages) into a beforeEach or API-level setup so the test body focuses on the single behavior under test. If UI setup is unavoidable, give it its own bounded helper with fail-fast assertions.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test times out late in a long inline setup, extract the setup or replace UI steps with API calls before adjusting timeouts.

learnings.title: Cumulative bounded waits silently exhaust test budget
learnings.detail: Tests that chain many precondition probes, fallback locators, and post-action readiness checks can stack their individual bounded waits high enough to exceed the overall test timeout, even when each step's timeout looks reasonable in isolation. A single slow path (animation, popup, slow navigation) tips the cumulative wait over the budget and the failure looks like a generic timeout rather than a specific stuck step.
learnings.specGuideline: Budget total wait time across all probes in a test, not per-step. Prefer shorter per-step timeouts (2-5s) with explicit waitForResponse/waitForURL anchors over many long fallbacks, and set the test timeout low enough that slow paths fail fast instead of silently consuming the full budget.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Sum the worst-case waits across all probes/fallbacks; if it approaches the test timeout, tighten per-step timeouts rather than raising the global one.

learnings.title: URL-prefix guards skip needed re-navigation
learnings.detail: Recovery logic that checks 'if already on /account, skip navigation' fails when the current page is a 404 or sub-route under that prefix where the expected sidebar/links don't exist. The guard treats URL membership as proof of correct state, but the DOM may be a not-found page or a nested route lacking the anchor element.
learnings.specGuideline: Don't gate re-navigation solely on URL prefix matching. Combine URL checks with a DOM readiness probe (e.g., the specific anchor or heading you need), and always re-check for 404/error markers after navigation completes.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a recovery path 'skips' navigation, verify the current page actually contains the target element rather than just matching a URL prefix.

learnings.title: Navigate after cart/mutation network confirms, not click
learnings.detail: After clicking Add-to-Cart or similar mutation triggers, navigating immediately to the resulting page (cart, checkout) often renders an empty/stale state because the underlying API call hasn't completed. The click resolves synchronously but the server-side mutation is still in flight.
learnings.specGuideline: Anchor post-mutation navigation on the network response (page.waitForResponse with the mutation endpoint) or on a UI signal that the cart/state count incremented, before navigating to the dependent page. Never rely on the click promise alone to mean the mutation finished.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If the destination page renders empty after an action, check whether the test waits for the mutation's network response before navigating.

learnings.title: Inconsistent locator filters across same test
learnings.detail: Using a strict locator filter (e.g., requires img + h3 + h2) for a second occurrence of an element that was successfully matched earlier with a looser filter (e.g., img only) causes the second lookup to fail on re-rendered pages where markup may vary slightly. The element exists but doesn't satisfy the stricter combined predicate within the wait window.
learnings.specGuideline: Reuse the exact same locator definition for repeated lookups of the same conceptual element within a test. Extract it to a helper or constant; don't tighten the filter on subsequent uses without a reason.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Diff the locator used at the failing step against earlier successful locators for the same element — align filters to the looser working version.

learnings.title: Locale-prefixed routes need full path on every navigation
learnings.detail: Apps with locale-prefixed routing (/us/, /dk/, etc.) often 404 when a recovery or fallback navigates to a bare /account or /cart path without preserving the locale prefix. The locale middleware may not redirect deep links consistently, and URL-prefix checks can mistake the 404 page for the correct location.
learnings.specGuideline: Always construct navigation URLs by prepending the active locale prefix detected from the current URL, including in fallback/recovery paths. Verify the destination doesn't 404 before continuing.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a navigation lands on a 404, check whether the locale prefix was preserved when building the target URL.

learnings.title: Readiness probes must repeat after re-render
learnings.detail: A waitForFunction or DOM readiness check that succeeded at the start of a test is often skipped on subsequent visits to the same page (e.g., navigating back after an action), even though the page has fully re-rendered and needs the same hydration/data-loaded signal. The second visit fails on a strict locator because the readiness gate was never re-applied.
learnings.specGuideline: Re-run the same readiness probe (waitForFunction, waitForLoadState, or content-based wait) every time you land on a dynamic page, not only on first arrival. Encapsulate the probe in a helper used at every navigation.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a locator works the first time but times out the second time on the same page, add the original readiness probe before the second locator call.

learnings.title: Avoid heading-tag assumptions for page titles
learnings.detail: Tests that lock onto h1 or specific data-testids for page titles frequently fail because apps render titles as h2, h3, or class-based divs. This is especially common on product/detail pages where designers use custom typography components instead of semantic headings.
learnings.specGuideline: Use broad title locators that accept h1-h3 plus common class patterns (product-title, page-title) and aria-level=1 roles. Never assume a specific heading tag exists without verifying via a smoke check first.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check the actual rendered markup for the title element — if no h1 exists, broaden to h2/h3/div with title-like classes rather than retrying the same selector.

learnings.title: Login forms often hide behind tabs or alternate routes
learnings.detail: Hardcoded /account/login or /login routes frequently 404 or render a marketing page; the actual credential form may be behind a Sign In tab, modal toggle, or at a locale-prefixed/unprefixed variant. Specs that wait for a username input on a single guessed URL exhaust their timeout before any fallback can recover.
learnings.specGuideline: Probe multiple known login route variants (with and without locale prefix) and click any Sign In tab/link/button before waiting on the username input. Use bounded per-route waits so the iteration completes within total budget.
learnings.fixerGuideline: First verify the login form actually renders at the configured URL by checking page body text; if it shows 'not found' or marketing copy, iterate route variants and reveal tabs before locating inputs.

learnings.title: Strict-mode .first() can resolve to hidden framework wrappers
learnings.detail: In Next.js, React, and other SSR frameworks, the first DOM match for a broad selector is often a hidden hydration wrapper or layout div with display:none, causing toBeVisible to fail even when the real content rendered correctly below it.
learnings.specGuideline: Always filter candidate locators to visible-only (locator.filter({ visible: true }) or :visible) before calling .first(). Prefer scoping to main/article/role-based containers over broad div selectors.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When toBeVisible fails on a .first() match, inspect whether the resolved element is a wrapper div with no dimensions — add a visibility filter rather than extending the timeout.

learnings.title: Race conditions between click and load-state on SPAs
learnings.detail: Promise.all([click, waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')]) resolves immediately on SPAs because the document is already loaded; reading page.url() right after returns the pre-navigation URL. This produces false 'link did not navigate' failures.
learnings.specGuideline: After clicking a link, await page.waitForURL with a predicate or page.waitForFunction that compares against the starting URL before asserting navigation occurred. Do not race click() against load-state events on client-routed apps.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a navigation assertion fails but the link clearly works manually, replace load-state waits with explicit URL-change waits.

learnings.title: Self-referential links break 'must navigate' assertions
learnings.detail: Footer/nav link audits often fail because some links point to the current page (href matches current path, fragment-only, or routes to the same locale root). The 'URL must change' assertion incorrectly flags these as broken navigation.
learnings.specGuideline: Before asserting a link changes the URL, compare the link's resolved href against the current page path and skip self-referential or fragment-only links from the navigation-change check while still verifying they don't error.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Inspect the failing link's href versus current URL — if they match (or differ only by hash), exclude it from navigation assertions rather than chasing a timing fix.

learnings.title: Checkout and cart flows need UI-driven seed, not direct nav
learnings.detail: Navigating directly to /checkout or /cart between tests loses cart state because Playwright contexts isolate storage. Specs that depend on prior test cart state fail because shipping options, line items, and totals never render on an empty cart.
learnings.specGuideline: Within each test that depends on cart contents, seed via UI (open PDP, select variant, click add-to-cart) before navigating to cart or checkout. Do not rely on cross-test state carryover or direct route entry to checkout.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If checkout assertions fail with empty form/no shipping options, verify the cart was seeded in this test — direct navigation to /checkout without prior add-to-cart is the usual cause.

learnings.title: Error-marker regexes need scope to main content
learnings.detail: Broad regex checks for 'not found', '404', or 'error' on a page often match navigation links, footer copy, or help-center text that legitimately contains those phrases, producing false-positive failures on working pages.
learnings.specGuideline: Scope error-marker locators to main, [role=alert], or heading elements with anchored regexes and exact-match filters. Never run a page-wide text search for error keywords without container scoping.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a 'page errored' assertion fails on a visibly working page, check whether the matched text lives in nav/footer — narrow the locator to main/alert/h1 scope.

learnings.title: Chained .or() locators can exceed budget on complex regexes
learnings.detail: Playwright's text-engine evaluates chained locator.or() variants serially, and regexes with special characters (currency symbols, accents) slow down resolution. A locator that should resolve instantly can time out under tight budgets.
learnings.specGuideline: For broad content presence checks (prices, status text, error states), prefer waitForFunction scanning visible text with a single compiled regex over long .or() chains of locators. Reserve .or() for 2-3 well-formed selectors.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a locator times out despite the content being visibly present, inspect for long .or() chains or regex with special chars and replace with a waitForFunction text scan.

learnings.title: Discover routes instead of hardcoding locale paths
learnings.detail: Tests repeatedly fail with 404s because hardcoded URL paths (locale prefixes, collection slugs, cart/checkout/login routes) don't match the actual app routes. Apps with i18n or dynamic catalog data rarely expose stable slugs, and guessing them produces brittle tests that break on any routing change.
learnings.specGuideline: Never hardcode deep route paths beyond the base URL. Discover navigation targets by clicking links from the homepage or by probing a small set of known route variants, and assert on the resulting URL with a loose regex rather than equality.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a test fails with 404 or empty-page errors, check whether the URL was hardcoded; replace with a link-click discovery flow or a route-variant probe loop.

learnings.title: Avoid chained .or() locators with broad regexes
learnings.detail: Chained .or() locators combined with broad regex labels and .filter({visible:true}) resolve very slowly in Playwright and frequently time out even when a matching element exists. The union locator evaluates every branch repeatedly, and a single slow branch blocks the whole wait.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer a bounded loop over candidate selectors with short per-candidate timeouts (1-2s each) instead of one giant chained .or(). Include data-testid, role+name, aria-label, placeholder, and href-based fallbacks as separate probes, and emit diagnostic info when none match.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If you see locator.waitFor timeouts on a union/.or() chain, refactor to a sequential candidate loop and add data-testid / aria-label variants.

learnings.title: Localized UI labels break English-only selectors
learnings.detail: Buttons and CTAs on localized storefronts use translated labels (e.g. Danish 'Læg i kurv' instead of 'Add to cart'), so English-only regex selectors never match even though the element is present and visible.
learnings.specGuideline: When the test runs against a non-default locale, include localized label variants in selectors or prefer locale-independent hooks (data-testid, role, itemprop, href patterns). Add a final DOM-level scan of visible buttons as a fallback.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check the BASE_URL locale segment; if non-English, add localized label variants or switch to data-testid/role selectors.

learnings.title: SPA login forms often live behind a tab or sub-route
learnings.detail: Many storefront/account pages render the sign-in form behind a tab, toggle, or dedicated /login sub-route rather than inline at /account. Tests that navigate directly to /account and wait for an email input hang because the form is never mounted on that route.
learnings.specGuideline: Build a reveal helper that (a) probes multiple known login route variants in order (/login first, then /account/login, then /account), and (b) clicks any Sign In tab/button/link before waiting for inputs. Verify the email input is actually visible before asserting on password/submit.
learnings.fixerGuideline: For auth timeouts, first check whether the form is gated behind a tab or a different sub-route; expand the route candidate list and add a tab-reveal step.

learnings.title: Wait for SPA hydration before declaring inputs absent
learnings.detail: Probing inputs with a 2s isVisible check causes false negatives on SPA routes where the form mounts lazily after hydration. The test bails on the correct route because it didn't wait long enough for client-side rendering.
learnings.specGuideline: When probing candidate routes, give each route at least 6-8s for inputs to mount before moving on. Use locator.waitFor({state:'visible', timeout}) per candidate rather than a single short isVisible() call.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If auth/route probes fail fast and never find the form, increase per-candidate visibility timeouts to allow SPA hydration.

learnings.title: Stateful pages require seeded prerequisites
learnings.detail: Direct navigation to cart, checkout, or address pages without first seeding cart contents or authentication lands on empty-state or redirect pages that lack the expected UI. Strict locators then time out on a page that legitimately doesn't render those elements.
learnings.specGuideline: Before testing cart/checkout/account flows, perform the prerequisite setup in-test: log in, add an item, then navigate via the in-app link (not direct URL) to preserve session and cart state. Broaden readiness checks to accept empty-state UI as a valid terminal state when prerequisites can't be met.
learnings.fixerGuideline: On checkout/cart/account timeouts, verify whether the test seeded the required state (auth, cart contents) before navigating; switch to link-based navigation from a populated state.

learnings.title: Price/CTA detection needs multi-signal fallbacks
learnings.detail: Narrow price selectors (specific classes, role=text with currency regex) miss real markup that uses itemprop, data-cost, amount classes, or locale-formatted currency strings (kr, DKK, €). A single-signal probe returns false on pages that clearly render the data.
learnings.specGuideline: Detect price and CTA presence using multiple orthogonal signals: structured attributes (itemprop, data-testid), class fragments (price, amount, cost), role+name, and a final text scan for currency symbols/codes appropriate to the locale.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When price/CTA assertions fail on a rendered page, inspect actual markup and add itemprop/data-* and currency-text fallbacks to the selector union.

learnings.title: Empty ARIA live regions need content-presence waits
learnings.detail: Asserting on role=alert visibility passes when the region mounts empty, but the text content arrives asynchronously (or renders elsewhere in the DOM). Tests fail because they check text immediately after visibility resolves.
learnings.specGuideline: Wait for the alert region to have non-empty textContent (or a specific substring) rather than just toBeVisible. Fall back to scanning body text when the alert role exists but renders no content, since some apps surface outcome messages outside the live region.
learnings.fixerGuideline: For alert/toast assertions that fail on empty content, replace toBeVisible with a waitForFunction on textContent length, plus a body-text fallback scan.

learnings.title: Strict mode hits hidden SSR wrappers
learnings.detail: Next.js and other SSR frameworks emit hidden wrapper divs (e.g. <div hidden>) around streamed content. Broad locators like getByRole('main') or [role=main] resolve to these wrappers first, so toBeVisible fails even though the page renders correctly.
learnings.specGuideline: Always chain .locator(':visible') or .filter({ visible: true }) onto landmark/region locators, or use .first() only after filtering out hidden ancestors. Prefer asserting on a specific visible child element rather than the outer landmark.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When toBeVisible fails on a landmark element, inspect whether the matched node is a hidden SSR/hydration wrapper and add a visibility filter.

learnings.title: Auth-gated tests need route+tab discovery
learnings.detail: Login and account routes vary across apps: /login, /account/login, /signin, or a tab inside an account shell. Hardcoding one path causes timeouts when the form lives elsewhere or is gated behind a 'Sign in' tab toggle.
learnings.specGuideline: Probe a list of candidate auth route variants and click any visible 'Sign in'/'Login' tab before waiting on email/password inputs. Only assert the form is found once a real input is visible; otherwise classify as a source bug rather than timing out.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a login input never appears, log the current URL and visible headings/tabs to confirm whether the route renders the form at all or hides it behind a toggle.

learnings.title: Cart/checkout requires awaited seed mutation
learnings.detail: Add-to-cart triggers an async network mutation. Navigating to /cart or /checkout immediately after click races the backend and renders an empty-cart view, causing downstream form/line-item assertions to fail.
learnings.specGuideline: Await the cart-mutation network response (page.waitForResponse on the cart API) after clicking add-to-cart, then allow client hydration before navigating. On checkout/cart pages, detect the empty-cart state and re-seed via UI before asserting form fields.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When checkout/cart assertions time out, first verify the cart was actually populated by checking the cart API response or the cart page's line-item count.

learnings.title: Add-to-cart CTAs vary widely in markup
learnings.detail: PDP add-to-cart buttons appear as <button>, <input type=submit>, form actions, or icon-only controls with localized/aria labels. Selectors keyed on the literal word 'cart'/'bag'/'basket' or English-only text miss many real implementations, especially when a variant (size/color) must be selected first to enable the CTA.
learnings.specGuideline: Use a broad candidate set: data-testid, aria-label, role=button with localized name regex, form[action*=cart] submit buttons, and text fallbacks. Select a variant option first if present, and check toBeEnabled (not just toBeVisible) before clicking.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Log all visible button labels and form actions on the PDP, and check whether a variant selector is gating the CTA's enabled state.

learnings.title: Chained .or() locators exceed budgets
learnings.detail: Long chained locator.or() expressions combined with :visible filters force Playwright to evaluate every branch on every poll, which is slow and frequently picks up hidden framework wrappers, exceeding per-locator timeouts.
learnings.specGuideline: Replace deep .or() chains with a sequential candidate loop: try each selector with a short per-step timeout (1-2s) and break on first visible match. Keep the overall budget bounded and prefer specific selectors over union expressions.
learnings.fixerGuideline: When a waitFor times out on a complex .or() locator, split it into sequential probes to identify which branch is slow or matches a hidden element.

learnings.title: Protected routes need server-side auth assertion
learnings.detail: Tests that assume logged-out users get redirected from account routes fail silently when the app lacks middleware/server guards — the route renders publicly or shows a shell with no redirect.
learnings.specGuideline: Assert the redirect via page.waitForURL with a login-pattern regex and a short timeout. If the URL stays on the protected route, mark the test as fixme with a SOURCE_BUG annotation rather than asserting on form visibility, since the bug is a missing auth guard.
learnings.fixerGuideline: Check the final URL and response status — if the protected route returns 200 with content for an unauthenticated session, it's an app bug, not a test issue.

learnings.title: waitForLoadState('load') unreliable on SPAs
learnings.detail: Client-side routed apps (Next.js App Router, React SPAs) often never fire the 'load' event after client navigation, causing waitForLoadState('load') to consume the entire test budget before any assertion runs.
learnings.specGuideline: Prefer waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded') or wait directly on a content selector. Remove redundant waitForLoadState calls between navigations and rely on element-visibility waits with bounded timeouts.
learnings.fixerGuideline: If a test exceeds its total timeout despite simple assertions, audit for waitForLoadState('load') or networkidle calls and replace with element-based waits.

learnings.title: Hardcoded fixture IDs and slugs cause 404s
learnings.detail: Tests that hardcode collection slugs, category paths, or entity IDs (order/product/user IDs) fail on environments where the fixture isn't seeded, returning 404 or empty pages.
learnings.specGuideline: Discover real entities by navigating from a stable entry point (homepage, listing page) and clicking the first available link, rather than hardcoding slugs. For detail-page tests, capture the ID from a list-page link or seed it deterministically via API.
learnings.fixerGuideline: On 404 or empty-page failures, check whether the URL contains a placeholder/hardcoded ID and replace with a discovery step from a known-good listing.
